source: A Multi-View Deep Learning Approach for Cross Domain User Modeling in Recommendation Systems

concepts.finding: Cross-domain recommendation using multi-view deep learning. User representation from multiple domains (movies, books, music) via separate autoencoders per domain. Shared hidden layer captures cross-domain user preferences. Addresses sparsity by transferring knowledge across domains.
concepts.implication: Cross-domain keyword intent: keywords from different domains (search, social, e-commerce) can be mapped to shared intent space. Multi-view learning handles domain-specific keyword patterns while sharing common intent structure.
concepts.open_question: Can keyword embeddings be improved by multi-task learning across domains (search, social, ads)? What constitutes different "views" of keyword intent?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Multi-view keyword embeddings (trained across search, social, and e-commerce domains) generalize better to new domains than single-domain embeddings.
